relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The seventh month opens with sacred rest and trumpet-blast.
A complete offering marks the month's awakening.
Flour and oil accompany bull and ram.
Each lamb receives its measured gift.
Purification accompanies the blast.
New-month worship remains joined to the daily offering.
The tenth day gathers the people into humility and cessation.
The Day of Atonement receives its complete offering.
Grain and oil accompany the larger animals.
Each lamb is counted.
Atonement layers upon continual worship rather than erasing it.
On the fifteenth day Sukkot begins in holy rest.
The first day opens with thirteen bulls and abundant offerings.
Every large gift receives its measured grain.
Fourteen lambs receive their portions.
Purification remains within harvest joy.
The second day reduces the bulls to twelve.
The measures continue with accountable order.
The daily offering remains beneath the festival abundance.
The third day brings eleven bulls.
The accompanying gifts follow their pattern.
Purification remains present.
The fourth day brings ten bulls.
Measured abundance continues.
The continual offering is not displaced.
The fifth day brings nine bulls.
Each gift retains its appointed measure.
Purification accompanies the day.
The sixth day brings eight bulls.
The pattern continues without haste.
Daily worship remains underneath festival worship.
The seventh day brings seven bulls.
The offering sequence reaches its seven-day completion.
Purification remains part of the whole.
The eighth day becomes a solemn gathering.
The offering contracts into one bull, one ram, and seven lambs.
The final day's measures are kept.
Purification and continual offering remain joined.
Appointed offerings stand beside vows, freewill gifts, and peace offerings.
Moses gives the full calendar to Israel.

contemplative_note: Numbers 29 moves through blast, humility, harvest abundance, and solemn closure. The descending offerings create a ritual exhale across Sukkot, while continual worship quietly remains beneath every extraordinary day.
